The renewing grace of God alone prepares the heart for every good work. This teaches us that we are not sufficient ourselves to think or speak anything wise and good.
Ignorance, pride, and self-flattery render us partial judges respecting our own conduct.
Roll the burden of your care upon God, and leave it with him, by faith and dependence on him.
God makes use of the wicked to execute righteous vengeance on each other; and he will be glorified by their destruction at last.
Though sinners strengthen themselves and one another, they shall not escape God's judgments.
By the mercy and truth of God in Christ Jesus, the sins of believers are taken away, and the power of sin is broken.
He who has all hearts in his hand, can make a man’s enemies to be at peace with him.
A small estate, honestly come by, will turn to better account than a great estate ill-gotten.
If people make God's glory their end, and his will their rule, he will direct their steps by his Spirit and grace. Let kings and judges of the earth be just, and rule in the fear of God.
To observe justice in dealings between person and person is God’s appointment.
The ruler who uses his power aright, will find that to be his best security.
Put those in power who know how to speak to the purpose.
Those are fools, who, to obtain the favor of an earthly prince, throw themselves out of God's favor.
There is joy and satisfaction of spirit, only in getting wisdom.
A sincerely religious person keeps at a distance from every appearance of evil. Happy is the person that walks in Christ, and is led by the Spirit of Christ.
When people defy God’s judgments, and think themselves far from them, it is a sign they are near. Let us not fear the pride of others, but fear pride in ourselves.
Humility, though it exposes to contempt in the world, is much better than high-spiritedness, which makes God an enemy. He who understands God’s word shall find good.
The person whose wisdom dwells in his heart, will be found more truly prudent than many who possess shining talents.
As waters to a thirsty land, so is a wise person to his friends and neighbors.
The wise person's self-knowledge always suggests something proper to be spoken to others.
The word of God cures the diseases that weaken our souls.
This is caution to all, to take heed of deceiving themselves as to their souls.
We must labor for the food which endures to everlasting life, or we must perish.
Ungodly people bestow more pains to do mischief than would be needful to do good. The whisperer separates friends: what a hateful, but how common a character!
Some do all the mischief they can by force and violence, and are blind to the result.
Old people especially should be found in the way of religion and godliness.
To overcome our own passions, requires more steady management than obtaining victory over an enemy.

All the disposal of Providence concerning our affairs, we must look upon to be determining what we referred to God; and we must be reconciled to them accordingly. Blessed are those that give themselves up to the will of God; for he knows what is good for them.
